objective: Compare costs of private and public universities, enrollment rates, and scholarship availability across 50 countries for international education policy analysis and studying abroad financial planning
sources: Global education data collection by Kathrina Roldan - tuition fees, university distribution, and enrollment statistics
features_description: Comprehensive international education finance dataset with tuition fees (min/max/average), university distribution (public vs private), student enrollment patterns, cost of living indices, and scholarship availability across 50 countries
representativity_description: 50 countries representing major global education markets with diverse economic and educational systems from developed and developing nations
sample_balance_description: Good geographic distribution covering Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Africa, and Middle East with diverse economic development levels

missing_values.description: Clean dataset with complete data for all 50 countries across all 16 variables

geographic_coverage.currency_normalization: All tuition fees converted to USD for comparison

education_structure.enrollment_patterns: Public vs private distribution varies significantly by country

column_details.description: Country name - 50 countries representing major global education markets
Reference year for all data - 2024 for consistency
Average private university tuition fee converted to USD for international comparison
Minimum private university tuition fee in USD
Maximum private university tuition fee in USD
Local currency used in each country before USD conversion
Total number of private universities in each country
Total number of public universities in each country
Percentage of universities that are privately owned vs total universities
Total enrollment in higher education (both public and private) in millions
Number of students enrolled in private universities
Number of students enrolled in public universities
Number of students in vocational/technical education programs
Population not participating in formal higher education
Relative cost of living index for affordability assessment
Percentage of students receiving financial aid or scholarships

comparative_analysis_capabilities.regional_comparisons: Americas vs Europe vs Asia-Pacific cost analysis
comparative_analysis_capabilities.economic_development: Developed vs developing nations education costs
comparative_analysis_capabilities.public_private_balance: Government vs private sector dominance patterns
comparative_analysis_capabilities.affordability_assessment: Tuition vs cost of living ratios

potential_applications: Study abroad destination selection and financial planning
Private vs public education sector analysis
Economic development impact on education costs
analytical_insights: Significant variation in tuition costs across countries (factor of 20x)
USA has highest absolute costs but strong private sector presence
European countries show mixed public-private models
Asian markets demonstrate rapid private sector growth
Cost of living index crucial for real affordability assessment
Scholarship availability varies dramatically by country
Private university percentage correlates with economic development
policy_implications: Need for international education cost transparency
Scholarship program design based on best practices

statistical_analysis_ready.regression_analysis: Predict tuition costs based on economic factors
statistical_analysis_ready.clustering_analysis: Country grouping by education system characteristics

data_visualization_opportunities: World map of tuition cost distribution
Public vs private university ratio by region
Cost of living vs tuition affordability scatter plots
limitations: Single year snapshot (2024) - no temporal trends
Limited to 50 countries - not comprehensive global coverage
Private university focus - limited public sector detail
Currency conversion point-in-time - exchange rate fluctuations
No quality metrics - cost only perspective
No graduate outcome or employment data
complementary_datasets: THE World University Rankings for quality metrics
OECD education statistics for additional context
World Bank development indicators for economic correlates
UNESCO education data for broader educational context
Graduate employment statistics for ROI analysis
machine_learning_applications: Tuition cost prediction models based on economic indicators
Country clustering by education system similarity
Outlier detection for exceptional education markets
Feature importance analysis for cost drivers
recommended_analysis_approach: Start with exploratory data analysis of cost distributions
Examine public-private university ratios by region
Analyze correlation between costs and economic indicators
Create affordability indices combining costs and living expenses
Develop predictive models for education market characteristics
